_comment: Shared XGID-decode contract corpus. Asserted by BOTH the Go decoder (pkg/blunderdb/domain/DecodeXGID, see xgid_contract_test.go) AND the GUI clipboard parser (frontend/src/services/importService.js parsePosition, see frontend/src/__tests__/xgidContract.test.js). The two implementations historically drifted (issue #13: Jacoby/Beaver flags, and the cube-value exponent vs actual-value mismatch); this corpus pins the shared, deterministic XGID-derived fields so any future divergence fails a test on at least one side. Conventions: color/owner 0=X(Black), 1=O(White), -1=None; cubeValueExp is the log2 exponent (0 -> cube shows 1, 2 -> shows 4); score is the AWAY score ([-1,-1] for money games). decision_type is intentionally NOT asserted: the GUI derives it from the analysis text while the Go decoder derives it from the dice, so they differ by design for a bare XGID. Edge cases avoided on purpose: turn=0 (Go->Black, JS->White) and match-play away score of 1 (JS applies a Crawford 1->0 remap the Go decoder does not).

cases.name: money game, Jacoby + Beaver, cube decision (issue #13 beaver position)
7pt match, cube owned by Black at exponent 2 (value 4), White on roll
money game, Jacoby only, checker decision
money game, Beaver only, White on roll, cube decision
